title: matelea tristiflora
intro: Matelea tristiflora, also known as Yellowthroat Vine, is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and relieving respiratory issues. Matelea tristiflora can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for its medicinal use, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically proven.
